attributes.action: Improvement of early pregnancy and maternity care
attributes.additional_details: The government should better support families in early pregnancy. NHS scans at 10–14 weeks could be supplemented with 6–8 week ultrasounds to confirm viability and detect complications sooner. Those with a prior miscarriage should have access to reassurance and progesterone where appropriate, as supported by evidence and NICE. Routine testing for antiphospholipid syndrome could also improve pregnancy safety.
attributes.background: Early ultrasound at 6–8 weeks aids timely viability confirmation and complication detection. Progesterone may be considered for women with early bleeding and prior miscarriage, while APS testing is usually reserved for recurrent pregnancy loss rather than routine first-scan screening.

departments.name: Department of Health and Social Care

rejection.details:
Your petition is about something that the Scottish Government is responsible for in Scotland. That means that the UK Government and Parliament can't look into it.  You may wish to resubmit your petition to the Scottish Parliament petition system:  
https://www.parliament.scot/get-involved/petitions 

There is a open petition on this petitions website calling for similar action which you may wish to sign:
https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/766594
